title: Billing Dispute Procedure
text: If you believe your electricity bill contains an error, you may raise a billing dispute. Disputes must be submitted within 30 days of the bill issue date. To raise a dispute: (1) Contact customer service through the self-service portal, mobile app, or by calling the customer service hotline. (2) Provide your account number, the bill number you wish to dispute, and a clear description of the discrepancy (e.g. incorrect meter reading, wrong tariff applied, unrecognised charge). (3) Our billing team will acknowledge your dispute within 2 business days and complete the investigation within 10 business days. (4) If an error is confirmed, a credit adjustment will be applied to your next bill. If the bill is found to be correct, we will provide a written explanation of each charge. You are not required to pay the disputed amount while the investigation is in progress, but you must continue to pay the undisputed portion of your bill to avoid late payment charges. If you are not satisfied with the outcome of the dispute, you may escalate the matter to the national energy ombudsman (RAE). Disputes submitted after 30 days will be assessed on a case-by-case basis.
